title: 10 best herbal tinctures for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for numbness in hands are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hands. These natural remedies help to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and relax nervous system tension, providing relief from numbness caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome, neuropathy, and poor nerve function. Examples include St. John's Wort, Ginkgo biloba, and Ashwagandha tinctures, which have been shown to improve hand dexterity, reduce pain and discomfort, and enhance overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as adenosine and caffeic acid, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve microcirculation, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the affected areas. This increased blood flow can help to alleviate numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hands, providing relief from symptoms associated with conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome and Raynaud's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Maidenhair tree tincture's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to numbness in hands by protecting nerve cells from damage.

Vasodilatory property: The tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels helps to improve circulation, increasing oxygen and nutrient supply to the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by reduced blood flow.

Neuroprotective property: Maidenhair tree contains compounds that have been shown to protect against neurodegeneration and promote nerve regeneration, making it potentially beneficial for treating numbness in hands caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or carpal tunnel syndrome.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: These bilavalent lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, potentially alleviating numbness in hands.

Flavonoids: Specifically, kaempferol and isorhamnetin, flavonoids found in Ginkgo biloba, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help protect the nerves from damage and improve blood flow, reducing numbness in hands.

Bilobalide: This triterpenoid saponin has been found to have neuroprotective properties, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of nerve damage, potentially alleviating numbness in hands by improving nerve function and blood flow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood circulation and alleviate numbness in hands.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain flavonoids and terpenoids that are thought to enhance blood flow and reduce numbness in the hands.
Barks: The bark of Ginkgo biloba is also used due to its flavonoid content, which may help in improving circulation and relieving numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in the fall when they are fully ripe and dry.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Steep 500g of chopped ginkgo biloba in 2 liters of 95% ethanol for 2-6 weeks in a dark glass container.
Strain the ginkgo biloba tincture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its remarkable ability to soothe and calm the nervous system. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often underlying causes of numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds help to relax muscle tension, improving blood flow and oxygenation to the extremities. This natural combination of actions helps to alleviate numbness and restore a sense of comfort and normal sensation in the hands.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic activity: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to numbness in hands by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands, which can cause numbness and tingling sensations by reducing oxidative stress and promoting blood flow.

Neuroprotective properties: Ashwagandha contains neuroprotective compounds that may help protect against damage to nerves and promote healthy nerve function, potentially alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by supporting nerve health and regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These alkaloids help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can cause nerve damage and numbness.

Withaferin A: This triterpenoid saponin is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect nerves from damage and alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Withanone: This withanolide has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which help to preserve nerve function and alleviate numbness and other symptoms associated with nerve damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which help in relieving numbness and pain.
Leaves: Leaves contain a significant amount of withanolides, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in reducing numbness and discomfort.
Seeds: Although less commonly used than roots, seeds of Withania somnifera contain withanolides and other compounds that may help alleviate numbness and pain when used in tincture form.
remedy_recipe: Harvest withania somnifera roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried withania somnifera roots and leaves with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain and discard the solids filter the liquid through a coffee filter into smaller glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves and joints. The curcuminoids present in turmeric also improve circulation and reduce oxidative stress, allowing for a more efficient transmission of nerve signals. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids, reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve endings, which can contribute to numbness in hands caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: The active compounds in turmeric, particularly curcumin, have natural pain-relieving and analgesic properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort associated with numbness.

Caryophyllene: As a terpene, caryophyllene exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce numbness in hands by blocking the pain signals to the brain and promoting nerve regeneration.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps with numbness in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes involved in inflammation, which in turn reduces numbness and pain associated with nerve damage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the primary source of curcumin, a key compound responsible for reducing inflammation and relieving numbness in hands.
Roots: As they contain a higher concentration of curcumin than rhyzomes, roots are often used to create effective tinctures for numbness in hands.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are used to create tinctures due to their high content of essential oils, which help alleviate numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa rhizomes by digging them up in the early morning or late evening.
Clean the rhizomes by washing them gently with cold water to remove any dirt.
Dry the cleaned rhizomes by placing them in a single layer on a paper bag for 1-2 weeks.
Steep the dried curcuma longa rhizomes in 80% vodka by weight at a ratio of 1:3 for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the liquid tincture by filtering it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pressure on nerves. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, also improve blood circulation to the extremities, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach nerve endings more efficiently. As a result, herbal ginger tinctures can effectively alleviate numbness in hands caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome, peripheral neuropathy, or Raynaud's disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can contribute to numbness in the hands by reducing pressure on nerves.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's ability to dilate blood vessels increases blood flow to affected areas, which can help restore oxygenation and nutrient delivery to nerves and tissues, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Ginger's analgesic compounds, such as gingerol and paradol, help reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands, making it easier for individuals to perform daily activities.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce numbness and pain in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly [6]-shogaol, exhibit vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects that help increase blood flow and reduce numbness in hands by relaxing blood vessels and reducing inflammation.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce numbness and pain in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and free radicals.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make tinctures for numbness relief, as they contain essential oils that help to reduce pain and inflammation.
Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale contain bioactive compounds that aid in pain relief and may be used in tinctures to help alleviate numbness in hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces to increase their surface area for optimal extraction.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 5 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to promote nerve health and improve blood circulation. The herb, also known as Bacopa monnieri, is traditionally used to enhance cognitive function and memory, but it also has a profound impact on the nervous system. By increasing the production of neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress, Brahmi tinctures can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, allowing for improved sensation and reduced discomfort. Regular use of Brahmi tincture may also lead to enhanced nerve function and reduced risk of nerve damage.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi tincture's ability to enhance cognitive function, including memory and concentration, can help alleviate numbness in hands by improving overall nervous system function and reducing symptoms of neuropathy.

Neuroprotective Properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds have been shown to prevent damage to nerve cells and promote their growth, which can help reduce numbness in hands caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or nerve damage.

Anti-Inflammatory and Antioxidant Properties: Brahmi tincture's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to numbness in hands by reducing damage to nerve cells and improving overall nervous system function.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides, a group of triterpene saponins found in Bacopa monnieri, may help with numbness in hands by enhancing nerve function and reducing oxidative stress that contributes to nerve damage.
Bacopasides: Bacopasides, another group of triterpene saponins similar to bacosides, may help alleviate numbness in hands by modulating neurotransmitter activity and improving nerve conduction.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpenoid saponin found in Bacopa monnieri, may help with numbness in hands by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that protect nerve cells from damage and promote nerve regeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of bacosides, which are known to improve nerve function and relieve numbness.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote nerve regeneration, helping to alleviate numbness in the hands.
Stems: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect nerves and reduce numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently brushing off any debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Combine 1 part dried bacopa monnieri with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to increase blood flow and circulation. The bioactive compounds in St John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve microcirculation, which can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate any underlying inflammation or irritation contributing to numbness, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being in the affected area.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to numbness in hands.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hypericin and hyperforin, can dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help increase oxygen and nutrient delivery to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.

Neuroprotective properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, including the ability to reduce inflammation in the nervous system and protect against neuronal damage, which can help alleviate numbness and other symptoms associated with neuropathy.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: They help to reduce numbness in hands by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which are involved in pain regulation.
Hypericin: It has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to protect nerve cells from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the hands, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Utilized for their flavonoid and lignan content, which may help relieve numbness and pain by reducing inflammation.
Flowers: Employed for their rich composition of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them completely in a warm place for 7 days.
Combine the dried hypericum flowers and leaves with 500ml of 45% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator to slow down the degradation process.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains a unique blend of herbal extracts that stimulate blood flow and circulation to the extremities. The potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds found in bell peppers work to soothe and calm irritated nerve endings, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the natural vasodilating properties help to widen blood vessels, allowing for increased oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the hands, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Bell peppers contain high levels of antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsaicin present in bell peppers has natural anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with numbness, allowing for improved blood flow and nerve function.

Neuroprotective compounds: Bell peppers contain neuroprotective compounds like vitamin C and carotenoids that help protect the nerves from damage and promote healthy nerve function, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin is a potent alkaloid that helps relieve numbness in hands by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thus reducing inflammation and pain.

Capsaicinoids (including dihydrocapsaicin and nordihydrocapsaicin): These capsaicinoids have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce numbness in hands by inhibiting the activity of certain pain receptors and reducing inflammation in the affected area.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids present in Capsicum annuum possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits, also known as peppers, are used due to their high capsaicin content, which helps to stimulate the nerve endings and relieve numbness.
Seeds: Capsicum seeds are used in tinctures because they contain a high concentration of capsaicin and other bioactive compounds that can help to reduce numbness and pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the Capsicum annuum plant are used in tinctures because they contain capsaicin and other compounds that can help to stimulate nerve endings and relieve numbness and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh capsicum annuum fruits and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Chop the cleaned capsicum annuum fruits into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar for 10 minutes.
Combine 250ml of 80% ethanol with 50ml of distilled water in a separate glass container and stir the mixture well.
Pour the ethanol and water mixture over the chopped capsicum annuum fruits in the jar and seal it tightly for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids, then store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, it reduces inflammation and swelling in the body, which can help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or nerve damage. The tincture also improves circulation, allowing oxygenated blood to reach the nerves more efficiently, which helps to reduce numbness and promote healthy nerve function.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main active compound, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids like licoricidin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect nerve cells from damage and promote healthy blood flow to the extremities, reducing numbness and tingling sensations.
Saponins: Saponins present in licorice root have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizic acid: It helps with numbness in hands by its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the hands, thereby relieving numbness.

Licoricidin (Glycyrrhizin): It helps with numbness in hands by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which reduces oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, leading to relief from numbness.

Licorisoflavan A: It helps with numbness in hands by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and inflammation in the nerves, thereby relieving numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and numbness.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and saponins that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to numb the hands.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain triterpenoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, reducing numbness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra for every 2 parts of alcohol by volume in a clean glass container.
Combine the harvested roots with 80 proof vodka or ethanol 190 proof as a solvent at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids.
Store the resulting herbal tincture in a dark glass bottle and keep it in the refrigerator to extend shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains a natural sedative property that soothes and calms the nervous system. By relaxing the nerves, valerian tincture can help reduce feelings of numbness, tingling, and pins and needles in the hands. remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for peripheral neuropathy.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which may contribute to numbness in hands.
Pain-relieving properties: Valerian has been shown to have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help relieve pain and numbness in hands.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps alleviate numbness in hands by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which may contribute to numbness.
Valeranone: Contributes to the relief of numbness by inhibiting the activity of voltage-gated sodium channels, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Isovaleric acid: May help alleviate numbness by enhancing the activity of GABA receptors, which can contribute to a calming effect and relief from numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, a compound responsible for the herb's sedative and numbing properties.
Barks: Valeriana officinalis barks contain bioactive compounds that contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate numbness and pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and other active compounds that have been traditionally used to treat numbness, pain, and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the valeriana officinalis roots in the fall when the plant is mature and the roots are at their peak potency.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with a gentle stream of water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a well-ventilated area using a food dehydrator or by spreading them out in a single layer.
Combine the dried roots with 80 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio by weight to create the tincture base.
Steep the tincture base in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking it every few days to enhance extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems in the morning to ensure maximum potency.
Clean the plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Use a 35% ethanol solution to create a tincture by placing 1 part plant material in 3 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a dark glass container with a lid, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol.
Combine harvested lavender with vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar to create a maceration mixture.
Store the maceration mixture in a cool dark place for a minimum of 4 weeks allowing for the tincture to steep and extract.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves according to the recommended dosage of 20% plant material by volume.
Cut the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with a menstruum such as vodka or ethanol at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass container for 4-6 weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Store the filtered tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years, using it as directed for numbness relief in hands.

remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried crataegus monogyna leaves and twigs to three parts vodka with an ethanol content of at least 70%.
Combine the herb mixture with vodka in a clean glass jar ensuring all surfaces are fully submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the tincture to macerate and extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Label and store the crataegus monogyna tincture in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to two years taking 20-30 drops twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its natural ability to promote blood flow and circulation. The antispasmodic properties of lemon balm help to relax and calm the nerves, reducing the tingling and numbness associated with poor circulation. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce swelling in the hands, further alleviating numbness. By taking a daily dose of herbal lemon balm tincture, individuals can experience relief from numbness and promote overall hand health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm tincture helps to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to numbness in the hands by calming down overactive nerve endings.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves and surrounding tissues, which can cause numbness, tingling, or burning sensations in the hands.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and free radical damage to nerve cells, which can contribute to numbness and other neurological symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for reducing numbness and pain in the hands.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have a high concentration of citral, a natural pain reliever that can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated and before flowering begins.
Rinse harvested material with clean water and pat dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Combine 50g of dried or 100g of fresh melissa officinalis material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking every 7-10 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass bottle using a dropper top.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains bioactive compounds that stimulate blood flow and circulation, thereby reducing numbness caused by poor circulation or nerve compression. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, ensuring they are organic and free of pesticides.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing with filtered water to remove any debris.
Combine 10 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with numbness in hands because it contains calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes and calms irritated nerves. remedy_parts: Roots: Potent in treating numbness, roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and promote circulation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and ensure they are free of contaminants.
Weigh out 250 grams of calendula flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Combine the calendula flowers with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka and stir well to combine.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom and dry them properly for 2 weeks.
Grind dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it to the top and stirring well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a new container, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible, discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots weighing approximately 500 grams or dried angelica sinensis root powder equivalent.
Clean and chop the harvested angelica sinensis roots into small pieces allowing for efficient solvent interaction.
Combine the chopped angelica sinensis roots with 70 percent ethanol at a ratio of one part herbs to four parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to macerate for eight hours or overnight under ambient temperature conditions with occasional stirring.
Strain and filter the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The herb, Scutellaria lateriflora, has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and insomnia, which can often manifest as numbness or tingling sensations in the extremities. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, skullcap tinctures can help alleviate numbness in hands by calming the nerves and improving circulation, allowing for a greater range of motion and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Skullcap's anxiolytic properties can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations caused by stress and anxiety, as excessive worry can lead to tension and vasoconstriction that restrict blood flow to the extremities.

Antispasmodic properties: Skullcap's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms that may contribute to numbness in the hands, allowing for improved blood circulation and nerve function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands and fingers, which can alleviate numbness caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Skullcap contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing numbness and pain in the hands.
Barks: The barks of Skullcap contain tannins and saponins, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scutellaria lateriflora flowers at peak bloom, collecting enough for the desired yield of dried plant material.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat, ensuring complete desiccation within 24 hours.
Combine one part dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers with two parts vodka or neutral grain spirits in a clean glass jar, filling to the top.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with date and ingredients.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome, nerve damage, and Raynaud's disease. Herbal tinctures like St. John's Wort, Ginkgo biloba, and Ginger root have been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of numbness, tingling, and cold intolerance in the hands and fingers. These herbal remedies may help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote nerve health, providing relief from discomfort and numbness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with numbness in hands is a blend of Ginkgo Biloba, Devil's Claw, and Rosemary. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood circulation to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling. Devil's Claw reduces inflammation and pain, while Rosemary enhances cognitive function and boosts circulation. This trio also promotes nerve regeneration and may help alleviate numbness caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome. Take 5-10 drops of each tincture, 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
